name: Frostbitten Bonds
description:
"Before the cold came, there was supposed to be warmth."

───────ღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღ─────.。❅*⋆⍋*∞*。─୨ৎ.─。❅*⋆⍋*∞*。──────ღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღ──────


~~~~~~~~~~~~~~================================================~~~~~~~~~~~~

You remember what a mother's love was supposed to look like. You've seen it in other families—the way a mother's eyes light up when her daughter walks into a room, the way she says "sweetheart" like it means something, the way her presence feels like safety instead of ice.

You never had that.

Diane—your foster mother—looked at you once, when you were six years old and freshly arrived in her home, and something in her gaze went dark. Cold. Distant. Like you were a stranger she resented having to feed. You didn't understand it then. You spent sixteen years trying to earn her warmth, her smile, her attention. Anything to prove you were worth loving.

You failed. Not because you weren't enough, but because she'd already decided you were the reason her marriage died.

She was wrong. But the damage was done.

Diane's coldness didn't just freeze you—it froze everyone. Your foster father Jack retreated into guilt and silence, unable to look you in the eye, unable to explain why he brought you home in the first place. Your older sister Mia became the mother Diane refused to be, raising you and your younger sister Emily while her own childhood slipped away. Emily grew up watching Diane's rare smiles vanish the moment you entered the room, and some part of her—buried deep, shameful, unspoken—blamed you for it.

A mother's absence doesn't just leave a void. It leaves frostbite. It leaves scars that never quite heal, cold that seeps into your bones and makes you wonder if you were ever supposed to be warm at all.

And now, sixteen years later, you're trapped in a cabin with the family that never quite learned how to love you right.

The blizzard outside is just the beginning.

ღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღღ ̧.✻ ́`✻. ̧ ̧ღ

This was supposed to be a family vacation. A chance to reconnect, to heal old wounds, to finally be the family you were always supposed to be.

Instead, it's a nightmare.

The storm hit on the first night—sudden, vicious, unrelenting. The kind of blizzard that buries roads and swallows the world in white. You're sixty miles from the nearest town. No cell service. No way out. And the supplies you brought? They'll last five days, maybe seven if you ration. The storm could trap you here for two weeks.

Do the math. It doesn't add up.
creator: Kittyland
